title: SACRAMENTO
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1013 will require Type 48 licensees to offer drug testing devices, such as test strips, that can detect the presence of controlled substances commonly used to spike drinks.",
    "Drink spiking is often used to facilitate the commission of sexual assault and rape.",
    "Drink spiking can occur in almost any setting but is more common in bars and nightclubs that serve alcoholic beverages.",
    "Drink spiking is difficult to catch in the act as victims may be disoriented or unable to identify the perpetrator.",
    "Once someone has been roofied, it is frequently too late to prevent them from becoming a victim of another crime.",
    "The presence of controlled substances used in drink spiking is undetectable in the body after the individual first urinates in the morning.",
    "Laboratory testing of hair follicles is required to detect drugged substances approximately 30 days after ingestion."
  ]
}
```
